content: Just as the language learning sense of reflexion (reflection) asks human learners to explicitly review their own output against implicit linguistic norms to reinforce mastery, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the scaffolded, educational reflexion framework from high school language writing workshops to improve their error correction ability. Specifically, structured, iterative reflexion exercises designed for human writing skill-building can be adapted to train LLMs to articulate inconsistencies between their generated outputs and desired standards more systematically, rather than relying on unstructured internal self-checks.
